components.name: sodium L-lactate
components.evidence: SI Appendix: 'strains were grown in MCLMAN minimal medium containing 0.5% partially purified gastric mucin and various carbon sources, including sodium pyruvate, sodium butyrate and L-lactate (1)'; main text: 'We tested C. jejuni growth in mucin-containing minimal media supplemented with either 10 mM butyrate or 10 mM L-lactate'

components.name: partially purified gastric mucin
components.evidence: SI Appendix: 'strains were grown in MCLMAN minimal medium containing 0.5% partially purified gastric mucin ... To determine the growth of C. jejuni strains in different concentrations of inorganic phosphate, strains were grown in 0.5% mucin containing MCLMAN minimal medium'

components.name: L-aspartic acid
components.evidence: MCLMAN (etymology M-C-L-M-A-N) 'contains inorganic salts, the amino acids l-cysteine, l-leucine, l-methionine and l-aspartic acid (nitrogen source), the vitamin niacinamide and lactate as a carbon source' (Alazzam et al., Res Microbiol 162:173-179, 2011); SI Appendix names the base as 'MCLMAN minimal medium'

components.name: L-cysteine
components.role: amino acid / sulfur source / reductant (MCLMAN base)
components.evidence: MCLMAN 'contains ... the amino acids l-cysteine, l-leucine, l-methionine and l-aspartic acid' (Alazzam 2011); the 'C' of MCLMAN

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: MCLMAN 'contains ... the amino acids l-cysteine, l-leucine, l-methionine and l-aspartic acid' (Alazzam 2011); the 'L' of MCLMAN

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: MCLMAN 'contains ... the amino acids l-cysteine, l-leucine, l-methionine and l-aspartic acid' (Alazzam 2011); the 'M' of MCLMAN. C. jejuni is methionine-auxotrophic (Tejera et al. 2020 M1cam study list methionine as an auxotrophic substrate at 4 mM in their MCLMAN-derived medium)

components.name: niacinamide
components.evidence: MCLMAN 'contains ... the vitamin niacinamide' (Alazzam 2011); the 'N' of MCLMAN. Tejera et al. 2020 (M1cam) list niacinamide at 33.1 uM in their MCLMAN-derived medium, but this value is not confirmed as the exact MCLMAN concentration

components.name: inorganic phosphate (phosphate buffer)
components.evidence: SI Appendix: 'To determine the growth of C. jejuni strains in different concentrations of inorganic phosphate, strains were grown in 0.5% mucin containing MCLMAN minimal medium' — confirms inorganic phosphate is a MCLMAN component (concentration varied experimentally; standard value in Alazzam 2011 not retrievable)

components.name: carbon dioxide
components.evidence: SI Appendix: 'All C. jejuni strains were routinely grown under microaerobic conditions (85% N2, 10% CO2, 5% O2)'

components.name: oxygen
components.evidence: SI Appendix: 'All C. jejuni strains were routinely grown under microaerobic conditions (85% N2, 10% CO2, 5% O2)'

components.name: MCLMAN inorganic salt / iron / trace-element base (unexpanded)
components.role: bulk salts + iron + trace elements (MCLMAN base, unresolved)
components.evidence: MCLMAN 'contains inorganic salts ...' (Alazzam 2011). The identities/concentrations of the individual salts, the iron source and any trace-element mix live only in the Alazzam 2011 Res Microbiol paper (10.1016/j.resmic.2010.09.024), which is closed-access and was not retrievable; NOT expanded here to avoid fabrication.

media.base_medium: MCLMAN minimal medium (Alazzam et al. 2011, Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168 defined minimal medium) supplemented with 0.5% (w/v) partially purified gastric mucin; carbon source 10 mM sodium L-lactate; microaerobic gas phase 85% N2 / 10% CO2 / 5% O2.

sources_chased.what: This paper's SI Appendix (Methods: Bacterial strains and media; Growth analysis in MH broth and minimal media)
sources_chased.how: curl NCBI OA supplementaryFiles zip (europepmc REST), extracted pnas.2316540120.sapp.pdf, pdftotext

sources_chased.what: MCLMAN base recipe (Alazzam B et al., Res Microbiol 162:173-179, 2011; DOI 10.1016/j.resmic.2010.09.024; PMID 21144899)
sources_chased.how: Attempted ScienceDirect, Elsevier, ResearchGate, academia.edu, EuropePMC, Semantic Scholar (openAccessPdf CLOSED), Unpaywall (oa_status closed), and 3 derivative papers (PMC7318876 M1cam GEM, PMC8531295 C. hepaticus, PMC7167465 Sinha 2020) — all paywalled or non-reproducing
added: confirmed MCLMAN component classes: 4 amino acids (Cys/Leu/Met/Asp), niacinamide, inorganic salts, lactate — but individual salt/iron/trace identities and ALL concentrations remain unresolved

sources_chased.what: Sinha et al. 2020 (PstSCAB, PMC7167465) = ref (1) of the SI carbon-source method
added: confirmed MCLMAN + carbon-source growth protocol and microaerobic gas mix; no mucin recipe here (mucin is novel to PMC10786315)

missing_before: MCLMAN inorganic salts + iron source + trace elements (still unresolved - closed-access Alazzam 2011)
media.notes: Grown microaerophilically (85% N2 / 10% CO2 / 5% O2), not fully aerobic; 'oxygen' kept per the model's O2/CO2 exchanges. The defined base is MCLMAN (Alazzam 2011): confirmed to contain 4 amino acids (L-Cys, L-Leu, L-Met, L-Asp), niacinamide, inorganic phosphate, unspecified inorganic salts + lactate. The complete salt/iron/trace table and every concentration exist only in the closed-access Alazzam 2011 Res Microbiol paper, which could not be obtained despite exhausting all open routes; those are listed by class and left unexpanded rather than fabricated. Everything the accessible literature confirms (mucin 0.5%, 10 mM L-lactate, gas phase, amino acids, niacinamide, phosphate) has been recovered.
